message:
feat(search): add tdestroy GNU extension (bd-srch-3)

Sub-bead 3 of bd-srch-epic. The previous fl-abi was missing the GNU
tdestroy(root, free_node) function entirely; this commit adds it as
a thin wrapper over RbTree::destroy_with from bd-srch-1.

API: pub unsafe extern "C" fn tdestroy(
    root: *mut c_void,
    free_node: Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(*mut c_void)>,
)

The GNU ABI takes the *value* of *rootp (not &rootp) so the caller
must NULL out their root pointer themselves after the call (matching
glibc's documented pattern). NULL root is a no-op; NULL free_node
drops the tree state without calling back per key.

3 conformance tests added (10 total in search harness):
- fl_tdestroy_calls_free_node_for_every_key: insert 10, count visits
  via thread-local cell
- fl_tdestroy_null_root_is_safe
- fl_tdestroy_with_null_callback_is_safe

Closes bd-srch-3; bd-srch-4 will move the hash table to core.
